_meta.review_warning: AI-drafted v0. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION until reviewed by a qualified Vic WHS practitioner and approved. Pool safety barrier compliance is governed by the Vic Building Regulations 2018 + AS 1926.1-2012; this SWMS is the work-safety doc, not the compliance doc.

hazards.title: Manual handling — glass / steel panels
hazards.risk: Glass panels heavy (typically 35–80kg) and awkward; steel panels similar weight

controls.title: Glass-lifting suction tools for all glass panels > 30kg
Two-person lift for all panels; planned route from delivery to position

hazards.title: Glass shatter — cuts / lacerations
hazards.risk: Toughened glass shattering during install if mishandled

controls.title: No tool strikes to edge; use rubber-faced wedges only

hazards.title: Hitting service when coring spigot footings
hazards.risk: Core drilling into paving / slab can hit unmarked services / waterproof layer

controls.title: BYDA + on-site scan before any coring
P2 respirator if dry coring (avoid where possible)

tasks: Pre-start: confirm barrier design complies with AS 1926.1 + Vic Building Regs (non-climbable zones, gap dimensions, gate self-close + self-latch).
Confirm site safety: any temporary barrier during install maintains effective enclosure if pool has water.
Set out spigot positions per design; check non-climbable zone clearance.
Core spigot holes (water-fed); flush and clean.
Set spigots in chemical anchor / grout per manufacturer.
Allow cure per spec before glass install.
Install glass panels using suction handles; two-person lift; rubber wedges only.
Install gates with self-closing / self-latching hardware; latch ≥ 1.5m from ground.
Compliance inspection: gap tests, non-climbable zone, latch height, self-close test 5 cycles.
Independent pool safety inspector engaged for Form 11 / certificate of compliance.
Certificate uploaded to Hub; copy to owner.
